ops.title: Re-run guides migration on captain-hook, cc-steer, cc-transcript when trees free up
ops.description:
Three repos deferred from the 2026-07-16 fleet-wide cc-guides re-migration (canonical_sha 7427f10241e99d2c9f6352528326fb0dabbabd08) because concurrent sessions held their trees:

- **captain-hook**: `cc-guides check` reports `.claude/settings.json` STALE — an active session has `settings-overrides.fragment.json` uncommitted-modified alongside source edits (cli.py, hooks.json, pipeline.py). Re-run after that session lands.
- **cc-steer**: `heads(::@- & bookmarks())` is empty — working copy parked on an unbookmarked feature stack (`feat(reactions)` + dirty deps bump). Re-run once back on trunk.
- **cc-transcript**: parked on feature bookmark `p1-workspace-split` (@- = pyi-stubs commit). Re-run once back on trunk.

Runbook: dispatch the saved workflow script `fleet-guides-migrate-fleet.js` (session 60dfd1d8, workflows/scripts/) with `args {"only": ["<repo>"]}` — contracts, whitelists, and gates are embedded. If the script is gone, the per-repo runbook + contract table live in the sweep note and the plan file. Preflight re-checks everything, so a still-busy tree just aborts again harmlessly.

ops.text: all three repos migrated: layout in template order, lock pinned to a canonical cc-skills sha, Guides CI green
